People who have grace will enjoy the instructions they receive. Those who suppress their beliefs are like animals.
A person who hides selfish and evil intentions behind a show of religion or friendship will be condemned.
Even though people may advance themselves through sinful actions, they cannot secure their position. But those who are rooted in Christ by faith are firmly established.

A wife who is pious, prudent, and manages her household well, who takes her duties seriously, and can endure hardship—such a woman is an honor and comfort to her husband. The opposite of this kind of woman drains and destroys him.
Thoughts are not private; they are known by God and therefore under His command. It is shameful for people to act with deceit, trickery, and scheming.
Unscrupulous people speak harmful things about their neighbors. A single good word can sometimes accomplish a great deal.
God’s blessing often continues to the families of godly people, while the wicked are overthrown.
The apostles showed wisdom by being proud of suffering shame for the name of Christ.
Someone who lives modestly, with no one to serve them, and earns their bread through hard work is happier than someone who boasts about high birth or fine clothes but lacks necessities.
A godly person would not inflict unnecessary pain on even an animal. But wicked people often speak well of others when they themselves wouldn’t tolerate such treatment for a single day.
It is wise to focus on your own business and pursue an honest occupation. But neglecting your responsibilities is foolish, and the grace of God teaches people to disdain nothing but sin.
When ungodly people see others prospering through sin, they wish they could act in the same way. But the root of divine grace in the hearts of the righteous produces different desires and goals.
Many a person has paid dearly for the words they have spoken.
When people use their words correctly to teach and comfort others, they experience acceptance through Christ Jesus; and their conscience assures them that they are fulfilling their purpose.
In Scripture, a fool is a wicked person—someone who acts contrary to wisdom from above. Their rule is to do what seems right in their own eyes.

A foolish person quickly becomes angry and expresses it rashly; they are constantly in trouble and getting into mischief. It’s beneficial for us to dismiss insults and offenses rather than dwelling on them.
It is good for everyone to fear and hate the sin of lying, and to be guided by honesty.
Gossip and suspicion, like a sword, separate those who were once close. The words of the wise heal and make people whole.
If truth is spoken, it will remain true; even if some are displeased, it will stand firm.
Deceit and falsehood bring fear and confusion. But those who seek the peace and happiness of others find joy in their own hearts.
If people are sincerely righteous, God has promised that no evil will happen to them. But those who delight in mischief will experience enough of it themselves.
Be honest not only in your words but also in your actions.
Foolish people openly proclaim the folly and emptiness of their minds.
People who avoid hard work in an honest occupation, living by tricks and dishonesty, are insignificant and impoverished.
Worry, fear, and sorrow weaken people’s ability to act or endure. A comforting word from God, applied through faith, brings joy to the heart.
The righteous person is abundant—not necessarily in material wealth, but in spiritual qualities and comforts, which are true riches. Evil people falsely flatter themselves that their ways are right.

A lazy person does not make good use of the opportunities that God provides and finds no satisfaction in them. The possessions of a diligent person, even if modest, benefit them and their family. They recognize that God has given it to them in answer to prayer.
The path of religion is straightforward; it is the way of righteousness. There is not only life at the end of the journey but also life along the way—true comfort.
